page: Microsoft Azure D64s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI) for accelerated vector calculations. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier performance in multi-core processing, as evidenced by strong ratings in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU evaluations. Memory performance is similarly robust, with large-block read and write bandwidth tests placing in the top 25%. Single-core performance, including single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core tests, falls within the average range. Cryptographic operations and database simulation benchmarks also demonstrate high efficiency, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D64s_v5 offers a dense allocation of compute and memory resources suitable for enterprise-scale workloads without the overhead of specialized GPU hardware. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly effective for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations. It is well-suited for production environments requiring consistent CPU performance, such as enterprise application servers, large-scale web serving, and relational database hosting.
description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks reveal strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth for large blocks, and excellent database operation throughput, while single-core metrics remain average. Supported by AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration, the D64s_v5 is highly efficient for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, including software compilation, static web serving, and database hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced 64-vCPU general-purpose instance delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
256 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or GPU accelerators
Strong multi-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth performance
Optimized for enterprise databases, software compilation, and web serving
